response: 359 is prime. It has no divisors among primes less than or equal to its square root (≈18.95), specifically 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, and 17.
reasoning_content: We need to check if 359 is prime. Quick sanity check: test divisibility by small primes. 359 mod 2: odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 3+5+9=17, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*51=357, remainder 2, so not divisible. 11: 11*32=352, remainder 7; 11*33=363 too high. 13: 13*27=351, remainder 8; 13*28=364 too high. 17: 17*21=357, remainder 2; 17*22=374 too high. 19: 19*18=342, remainder 17; 19*19=361, remainder -2, so not. 23: 23*15=345, remainder 14; 23*16=368 too high. 29: 29*12=348, remainder 11; 29*13=377 too high. 31: 31*11=341, remainder 18; 31*12=372 too high. 37: 37*9=333, remainder 26; 37*10=370 too high. 41: 41*8=328, remainder 31; 41*9=369 too high. 43: 43*8=344, remainder 15; 43*9=387 too high. We only need to test up to sqrt(359) ≈ 18.95, so primes up to 19. Actually sqrt is about 18.95, so we test primes up to 17 (since 19 > sqrt). So we tested 2,3,5,7,11,13,17. None divide. So 359 is prime. Quick sanity check: yes. Answer: prime.
